leagueBriefing: Conference tournament season reached a fever pitch on Sunday as heavyweights collided for silverware. Texas made a massive statement by dethroning South Carolina in the SEC, while UCLA left no doubt about their dominance in the Big Ten. Meanwhile, UConn continued its march toward another title with a century-mark performance in the Big East semifinals.

games.headline: Texas ends South Carolina’s reign to claim SEC crown
games.brief: In a battle of titans, Texas proved they are the team to beat heading into the Big Dance by dismantling South Carolina. The Longhorns' defense swamped the Gamecocks from the opening tip, building a double-digit lead that the defending champs simply couldn't chip away at. It’s a performance that sends shockwaves through the bracket projections and establishes Texas as a bona fide national title favorite.
bullets: Texas snapped South Carolina's long-standing dominance in conference tournament play.
The Longhorns led by as many as 21 points during a clinical second half.
South Carolina struggled with uncharacteristic turnovers and poor shooting percentages.
Madison Booker led the way for Texas, controlling the tempo and hitting clutch buckets.
games.context: Texas secures the SEC's automatic bid and likely a #1 overall seed in the NCAA Tournament.

highlights.title: Texas vs. South Carolina Game Highlights

games.headline: UCLA smothers Iowa to capture Big Ten title
games.brief: UCLA didn't just win the Big Ten Tournament; they made a statement that echoed across the country. The Bruins' defense was suffocating, holding a high-powered Iowa offense to a meager 45 points in a blowout of historic proportions. From the first whistle, UCLA's length and athleticism were too much for the Hawkeyes to handle, turning the championship game into a highlight reel for the Bruins.
bullets: The 51-point margin is one of the largest in Big Ten Tournament final history.
UCLA held Iowa to under 10 points in both the first and fourth quarters.
Lauren Betts dominated the paint on both ends of the floor for the Bruins.
Iowa finished with their lowest scoring output of the entire season.
games.context: UCLA enters the NCAA Tournament with massive momentum as Big Ten Champions.

highlights.title: Iowa vs. UCLA Big Ten Final Highlights

games.headline: Duke outlasts Louisville in overtime thriller for ACC glory
games.brief: It took five extra minutes to decide the ACC champion, but Duke eventually stood tall after an exhausting battle with Louisville. In a game defined by lead changes and physical defense, the Blue Devils found an extra gear in overtime to pull away. It was a gritty, high-stakes encounter that felt like a Final Four preview, with Duke proving they have the poise to win when the pressure is at its highest.
bullets: Duke claimed their first ACC Tournament title since 2013.
The game saw 12 lead changes before Duke took control in the overtime period.
Louisville's comeback effort in the fourth quarter forced the extra session.
Reigan Richardson came up big for the Blue Devils with key baskets in OT.
games.context: Duke secures the ACC's automatic bid and a likely top-4 seed in the NCAA Tournament.

highlights.title: Louisville vs. Duke ACC Final Highlights

games.headline: Sarah Strong leads UConn's hundred-point rout of Creighton
games.brief: UConn looked every bit the top-ranked team in the nation on Sunday, dismantling Creighton to punch their ticket to the Big East title game. Sarah Strong was everywhere, putting up a stat line that looked more like a video game than a semifinal. The Huskies' ball movement and transition game were too fast for Creighton, as UConn hit the century mark and reminded the field why they are the favorites to cut down the nets in April.
bullets: Freshman Sarah Strong dropped 23 points, 7 rebounds, and 6 steals.
UConn reached 100 points for the fifth time this season.
The Huskies' defense forced 19 turnovers, leading to 28 transition points.
Creighton was held to just 9 points in a lopsided opening quarter.
games.context: UConn advances to the Big East Championship game seeking their 22nd tournament title.

games.headline: West Virginia grinds out Big 12 title against TCU
games.brief: In a defensive slugfest that came down to the final minutes, West Virginia showed more grit to claim the Big 12 Tournament title over TCU. The Mountaineers' relentless pressure eventually wore down the Horned Frogs, who struggled to find clean looks in the fourth quarter. It wasn't the prettiest offensive display, but for West Virginia fans, the trophy looks exactly the same after a hard-fought battle in Kansas City.
bullets: West Virginia's defense held TCU to just 9 points in the critical third quarter.
JJ Quinerly led the Mountaineers with 18 points and key defensive stops.
This marks West Virginia's first Big 12 tournament championship since 2017.
TCU was held well below their season scoring average of 74 points.
games.context: West Virginia earns the Big 12 automatic bid and a major boost in seeding.

highlights.title: WVU vs. TCU Big 12 Final Highlights

games.leagueBriefing: Tournament madness was in full swing on Sunday as underdogs shook up conference brackets across the country. Air Force pulled off the shocker of the day by toppling top-seeded San Diego State, while Samford completed a stunning rally to snatch the SoCon title. From overtime survival in the MAAC to dominant displays in the Sun Belt, the road to the Big Dance is getting wild.

games.headline: Huerter and Andersen power No. 25 Fairfield into MAAC title game
games.brief: Fairfield's dynamic duo of Jillian Huerter and Meghan Andersen proved too much for Merrimack, as the Stags pulled away in the second half to secure their spot in the MAAC championship. Despite a cold night from deep for Andersen, her presence inside complemented Huerter's hot hand from the perimeter. The Stags survived a mid-game lull to maintain their quest for a conference sweep.
bullets: Jillian Huerter led the Stags with 19 points, including five triples.
Fairfield held Merrimack to just 7 points in the final quarter to seal the win.
The Stags' defense neutralized Merrimack's leading scorers after a tight first half.
Fairfield advances to face top-seeded Quinnipiac for the tournament trophy.
games.context: Fairfield moves one win away from an automatic NCAA Tournament bid.

games.headline: Samford stuns Chattanooga to punch ticket to March Madness
games.brief: In an improbable underdog story, Samford erased an early double-digit deficit to upset top-seeded Chattanooga and capture the Southern Conference title. Behind a barrage of three-pointers and ice-cold free-throw shooting in the final seconds, the Bulldogs secured an automatic NCAA bid in head coach Matt Wise's debut season. It was a classic tournament turnaround for a team that found its best form at exactly the right time.
bullets: Freshman Kaylee Yarbrough came off the bench for a career-high 25 points.
Samford shot a blistering 58.8% (10-for-17) from beyond the arc.
The Bulldogs converted 12-of-13 free throws to fend off a late Mocs rally.
This marks Samford's first SoCon championship under their new coaching staff.
games.context: Samford earns the SoCon automatic bid and their first NCAA appearance since 2020.

games.headline: Quinnipiac survives Iona in overtime thriller to reach MAAC final
games.brief: Top-seeded Quinnipiac narrowly avoided disaster, outlasting a gritty Iona squad in an overtime battle that came down to the final possession. After Iona took a late lead in the extra period, the Bobcats found a way to reclaim the edge and watched as the Gaels' final attempt missed the mark. It was a game of punch and counter-punch that kept the Atlantic City crowd on its feet until the buzzer.
bullets: Karson Martin led the Bobcats with 19 points off the bench.
Iona's Zoey Ward recorded a double-double with 23 points and 10 rebounds.
The game featured multiple lead changes in both the fourth quarter and OT.
Quinnipiac dominated the glass with a 43-32 rebounding advantage.
games.context: The Bobcats advance to face Fairfield in a heavyweight MAAC Championship clash.

games.headline: James Madison dominates top-seeded Georgia Southern to reach Sun Belt final
games.brief: James Madison made a mockery of the 'underdog' label, crushing top-seeded Georgia Southern in the Sun Belt semifinals. The Dukes used a massive rebounding advantage and a stifling defense to hold the regular-season champs to just 53 points. Peyton McDaniel was the engine for JMU, putting on a scoring clinic that left the Eagles searching for answers as the Dukes booked their fourth consecutive trip to the title game.
bullets: Peyton McDaniel dazzled with 25 points, her fourth 25+ point game of the season.
JMU dominated the boards with a staggering 45-16 rebounding margin.
The Dukes held Sun Belt Player of the Year Kishyah Anderson to just 8 points.
JMU is the first 4-seed to reach the SBC title game since 2016.
games.context: JMU advances to the Sun Belt Championship game with a bid to the NCAA Tournament on the line.

games.headline: Purdue Fort Wayne shoots lights out in blowout win over IU Indy
games.brief: Purdue Fort Wayne delivered an offensive masterclass, posting one of the best shooting percentages in Horizon League history to dismiss IU Indianapolis. The Mastodons couldn't miss in the second and third quarters, turning a close game into a rout in minutes. With balanced scoring and a dominant effort on the glass, PFW looks like a dangerous contender heading into the tournament semifinals.
bullets: The Mastodons' 60.9% field goal mark is the 3rd-best in HL tournament history.
Hillary Offing led a balanced attack where every Mastodon player scored.
A 14-2 second-quarter run sparked by Destiny Macharia broke the game open.
PFW secured their third consecutive 20-win season with the victory.
games.context: PFW advances to face top-seeded Green Bay in the Horizon League semifinals.

games.headline: Idaho extends record streak to 16 wins in Big Sky quarterfinal
games.brief: The Idaho Vandals continued their historic run, overcoming a spirited Weber State team to push their winning streak to 16 games. After a back-and-forth opening quarter, Idaho's defense clamped down, holding the Wildcats without a field goal for over six minutes in the second. The Vandals' depth and defensive discipline were the difference as they booked their first semifinal appearance in four years.
bullets: The 16-game win streak sets a new program record for the NCAA era.
Ana Pinheiro recorded a double-double and tied her career high in rebounds.
Idaho outrebounded Weber State by 19 in a dominant interior performance.
Kyra Gardner led the defensive effort with a season-high 8 steals.
games.context: Top-seeded Idaho advances to the Big Sky semifinals as the tournament favorite.

games.headline: Williamson's double-double carries Oregon State past Portland
games.brief: Lizzy Williamson was a force of nature in the WCC quarterfinals, leading Oregon State to a gritty win over Portland. The Beavers dominated the interior, out-rebounding the Pilots by 25 and controlling the paint from start to finish. While Portland managed to keep the score respectable with perimeter play, they simply had no answer for Williamson's size and efficiency under the basket.
bullets: Lizzy Williamson posted 20 points, 16 rebounds, and 3 blocks.
Oregon State held a massive 54-29 advantage on the boards.
The Beavers recorded 20 second-chance points compared to Portland's 9.
OSU advances to face No. 1 seed LMU in the tournament semifinals.
games.context: Oregon State moves closer to a WCC title shot and a potential NCAA berth.

games.headline: Troy fends off Arkansas State rally to secure Sun Belt final berth
games.brief: In a high-scoring Sun Belt semifinal, Troy relied on a massive second-quarter surge to build a cushion they would need to survive an Arkansas State comeback. The Red Wolves cut a 19-point lead down to single digits in the fourth quarter, but Troy's execution down the stretch kept the upset at bay. It was a physical, fast-paced game that showcased why these two teams were among the league's elite all year.
bullets: Troy outscored Arkansas State 20-7 in the pivotal second quarter.
Arkansas State's D’Arrah Allen led all scorers with 18 points.
A late 10-0 run for Troy in the fourth quarter effectively ended the comeback.
Troy forced 18 turnovers, turning them into 21 transition points.
games.context: Troy advances to face James Madison in a battle for the Sun Belt championship.

games.headline: Air Force pulls massive upset to bounce top-seeded Aztecs from MW tourney
games.brief: The Mountain West tournament was turned upside down as Air Force stunned regular-season champion San Diego State. Despite a decorated Aztecs squad featuring the Coach and Player of the Year, the Falcons found another gear in the second half, out-executing the favorites in the final minutes. It's a devastating blow for an SDSU team that dominated the league all winter but now faces an anxious wait for a potential at-large bid.
bullets: Air Force overcame a halftime deficit to score 51 points in the second half.
San Diego State's Nala Williams (MW Player of the Year) was held in check late.
The Falcons' transition offense repeatedly caught the Aztecs out of position.
This is Air Force's first-ever tournament win over a No. 1 seed.
games.context: San Diego State's automatic bid hopes are dashed, while Air Force becomes the tourney's Cinderella.
